--- test_cfgpipe.py
+++ test_cfgpipe.py
@@ -0,0 +1,606 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3

+"""Tests for cfgpipe - Configuration pipeline with groups and custom types."""

+

+import os

+import sys

+import json

+import tempfile

+import unittest

+

+sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))

+

+from cfgpipe import Config, SchemaError, ResolutionError, register_type

+from cfgpipe.types import Type, PortType

+

+

+class TestNestedGroups(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test examples with nested groups."""

+

+    def test_example_61_nested_groups(self):

+        """6.1 Nested Groups (env: DB_HOST=proddb.internal)"""

+        schema = {

+            "server": {

+                "host": {"type": "string", "default": "localhost", "env": "SERVER_HOST"},

+                "port": {"type": "integer", "default": "8080"}

+            },

+            "db": {

+                "connection": {

+                    "host": {"type": "string", "env": "DB_HOST"},

+                    "port": {"type": "integer", "default": "5432"}

+                }

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+

+        # With env vars

+        env = {"DB_HOST": "proddb.internal"}

+        result = config.resolve(env=env)

+

+        expected = {

+            "server": {"host": "localhost", "port": "8080"},

+            "db": {"connection": {"host": "proddb.internal", "port": "5432"}}

+        }

+        self.assertEqual(result, expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {result}")

+

+        # Without env vars - should use defaults

+        result2 = config.resolve()

+        expected2 = {

+            "server": {"host": "localhost", "port": "8080"},

+            "db": {"connection": {"port": "5432"}}

+        }

+        self.assertEqual(result2, expected2, f"Expected {expected2}, got {result2}")

+

+    def test_uses_composed_paths_in_errors(self):

+        """Verify that errors use full composed paths."""

+        schema = {

+            "server": {

+                "host": {"type": "not-a-type", "default": "localhost"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("server.host", str(cm.exception))

+

+

+class TestCustomTypePort(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test custom port type validation."""

+

+    def test_example_62_port_validation_failure(self):

+        """6.2 Custom Type Validation Failure (env: LISTEN_PORT=99999)"""

+        schema = {

+            "server": {

+                "listen_port": {"type": "port", "env": "LISTEN_PORT"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+

+        # Invalid port number

+        env = {"LISTEN_PORT": "99999"}

+

+        with self.assertRaises(ResolutionError) as cm:

+            config.resolve(env=env)

+

+        self.assertEqual(cm.exception.path, "server.listen_port")

+        self.assertEqual(cm.exception.source, "env")

+        self.assertIn("port out of range", cm.exception.detail)

+

+    def test_port_valid_range(self):

+        """Test valid port numbers."""

+        schema = {

+            "server": {

+                "port": {"type": "port", "default": "8080"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["server"]["port"], "8080")

+

+    def test_port_edge_cases(self):

+        """Test edge cases for port type."""

+        schema = {

+            "app": {

+                "port0": {"type": "port", "default": "0"},

+                "port65535": {"type": "port", "default": "65535"},

+                "port1": {"type": "port", "default": "1"},

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["port0"], "0")

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["port65535"], "65535")

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["port1"], "1")

+

+    def test_port_leading_zeros(self):

+        """Test that leading zeros are rejected."""

+        schema = {

+            "app": {

+                "port": {"type": "port", "default": "08080"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+

+        with self.assertRaises(ResolutionError) as cm:

+            config.resolve()

+

+        self.assertEqual(cm.exception.path, "app.port")

+        self.assertIn("leading zeros", cm.exception.detail)

+

+

+class TestGroupSourceAnnotation(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test validation of source annotations on groups."""

+

+    def test_example_63_group_carries_source_annotation(self):

+        """6.3 Group Carries a Source Annotation"""

+        schema = {

+            "cache": {

+                "env": "CACHE_CONFIG",  # Invalid: group carries source annotation

+                "ttl": {"type": "integer", "default": "300"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("cache", str(cm.exception))

+        self.assertIn("source-annotation", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_group_with_env_key(self):

+        """Test various source keys on groups."""

+        for key in ["default", "env", "file", "arg", "primary-store"]:

+            schema = {

+                "group": {

+                    key: "some_value",

+                    "param": {"type": "string"}

+                }

+            }

+

+            with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+                Config(schema)

+

+            self.assertIn("group", str(cm.exception))

+

+

+class TestDuplicatePrimaryStore(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test duplicate primary-store detection."""

+

+    def test_example_64_duplicate_primary_store(self):

+        """6.4 Duplicate Primary-Store Keys Across Groups"""

+        schema = {

+            "frontend": {

+                "timeout": {"type": "integer", "primary-store": "service/timeout"}

+            },

+            "backend": {

+                "timeout": {"type": "integer", "primary-store": "service/timeout"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        error_msg = str(cm.exception)

+        self.assertIn("frontend.timeout", error_msg)

+        self.assertIn("backend.timeout", error_msg)

+        self.assertIn("service/timeout", error_msg)

+        self.assertIn("duplicate", error_msg.lower())

+

+    def test_duplicate_primary_store_same_group(self):

+        """Test duplicate primary-store in same group."""

+        schema = {

+            "service": {

+                "timeout": {"type": "integer", "primary-store": "app/timeout"},

+                "retries": {"type": "integer", "primary-store": "app/timeout"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("service.timeout", str(cm.exception))

+        self.assertIn("service.retries", str(cm.exception))

+

+

+class TestUnrecognizedType(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test unrecognized type error."""

+

+    def test_example_65_unrecognized_type(self):

+        """6.5 Unrecognized Type in a Nested Group"""

+        schema = {

+            "metrics": {

+                "endpoint": {"type": "url", "default": "http://localhost:9090"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("metrics.endpoint", str(cm.exception))

+        self.assertIn("url", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_unrecognized_type_at_root(self):

+        """Test unrecognized type at root level."""

+        schema = {

+            "param": {"type": "foo", "default": "bar"}

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("param", str(cm.exception))

+        self.assertIn("foo", str(cm.exception))

+

+

+class TestSchemaValidation(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test various schema validation scenarios."""

+

+    def test_empty_schema(self):

+        """Test empty schema is invalid."""

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config({})

+

+        self.assertIn("non-empty", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_null_schema(self):

+        """Test null schema is invalid."""

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(None)  # type: ignore

+

+        self.assertIn("object", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_scalar_schema(self):

+        """Test scalar schema is invalid."""

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config("not an object")  # type: ignore

+

+        self.assertIn("object", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_array_schema(self):

+        """Test array schema is invalid."""

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config([{}])  # type: ignore

+

+        self.assertIn("object", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_group_defines_own_type(self):

+        """Test that group cannot define type for itself with string value."""

+        schema = {

+            "server": {

+                "type": "string",  # Invalid for group

+                "host": {"type": "string"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("server", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_group_defines_own_type_with_object_value(self):

+        """Test that group CAN define type for itself if value is not a string (object)."""

+        schema = {

+            "server": {

+                "type": {"nested": "object"},  # Not a string - allowed as object value

+                "host": {"type": "string"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        # This should work - type is an object, not a string

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+        self.assertEqual(result["host"], None)  # No default for host

+

+    def test_entry_not_object_valued(self):

+        """Test that group entries must be object-valued."""

+        schema = {

+            "server": {

+                "port": "not-an-object"

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("server.port", str(cm.exception))

+

+    def test_group_with_non_string_source_value(self):

+        """Test that group source annotations must be string values."""

+        schema = {

+            "cache": {

+                "env": 123,  # Not a string

+                "ttl": {"type": "integer"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        with self.assertRaises(SchemaError) as cm:

+            Config(schema)

+

+        self.assertIn("cache", str(cm.exception))

+

+

+class TestSourceResolution(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test parameter source resolution."""

+

+    def test_default_source(self):

+        """Test default value resolution."""

+        schema = {

+            "app": {

+                "port": {"type": "integer", "default": "3000"},

+                "host": {"type": "string", "default": "localhost"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["port"], "3000")

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["host"], "localhost")

+

+    def test_env_source(self):

+        """Test environment variable resolution."""

+        schema = {

+            "app": {

+                "port": {"type": "integer", "default": "3000", "env": "APP_PORT"},

+                "host": {"type": "string", "env": "APP_HOST"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve(env={"APP_PORT": "4000", "APP_HOST": "example.com"})

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["port"], "4000")

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["host"], "example.com")

+

+    def test_arg_source(self):

+        """Test command-line argument resolution."""

+        schema = {

+            "app": {

+                "port": {"type": "integer", "default": "3000", "arg": "--port"},

+                "host": {"type": "string", "arg": "--host"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve(cli_args={"--port": "5000", "--host": "test.com"})

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["port"], "5000")

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["host"], "test.com")

+

+    def test_file_source(self):

+        """Test file content resolution."""

+        schema = {

+            "app": {

+                "config": {"type": "string", "file": "config.txt"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve(file_contents={"config.txt": "some config data"})

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["config"], "some config data")

+

+    def test_priority_order(self):

+        """Test that arg > env > file > default priority is respected."""

+        schema = {

+            "app": {

+                "value": {

+                    "type": "integer",

+                    "default": "1",

+                    "env": "APP_VAL",

+                    "file": "config.txt",

+                    "arg": "--val"

+                }

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+

+        # Arg should win

+        result = config.resolve(

+            cli_args={"--val": "4"},

+            env={"APP_VAL": "2"},

+            file_contents={"config.txt": "3"}

+        )

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["value"], "4")

+

+        # Without arg, env should win

+        result = config.resolve(

+            env={"APP_VAL": "2"},

+            file_contents={"config.txt": "3"}

+        )

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["value"], "2")

+

+        # Without env, file should win

+        result = config.resolve(file_contents={"config.txt": "3"})

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["value"], "3")

+

+        # Without any source, default

+        result = config.resolve()

+        self.assertEqual(result["app"]["value"], "1")

+

+

+class TestBuiltInTypes(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test built-in parameter types."""

+

+    def test_string_type(self):

+        """Test string type."""

+        schema = {"param": {"type": "string", "default": "hello"}}

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+        self.assertEqual(result["param"], "hello")

+

+    def test_integer_type(self):

+        """Test integer type."""

+        schema = {"param": {"type": "integer", "default": "42"}}

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+        self.assertEqual(result["param"], 42)

+

+    def test_integer_parse_error(self):

+        """Test integer parse error."""

+        schema = {"param": {"type": "integer", "default": "not-an-int"}}

+        config = Config(schema)

+

+        with self.assertRaises(ResolutionError):

+            config.resolve()

+

+    def test_float_type(self):

+        """Test float type."""

+        schema = {"param": {"type": "float", "default": "3.14"}}

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+        self.assertEqual(result["param"], 3.14)

+

+    def test_boolean_type(self):

+        """Test boolean type with various representations."""

+        schema = {"param": {"type": "boolean", "default": "true"}}

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+        self.assertTrue(result["param"])

+

+        # Test false

+        schema = {"param": {"type": "boolean", "default": "false"}}

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+        self.assertFalse(result["param"])

+

+    def test_boolean_values(self):

+        """Test all boolean representations."""

+        true_values = ["true", "1", "yes", "on", "TRUE", "Yes"]

+        false_values = ["false", "0", "no", "off", "FALSE", "No"]

+

+        schema = {"param": {"type": "boolean", "default": "false"}}

+        config = Config(schema)

+

+        for val in true_values:

+            result = config.resolve(cli_args={"param": val})

+            self.assertEqual(result["param"], val.lower(), f"Failed for {val}")

+

+        for val in false_values:

+            result = config.resolve(cli_args={"param": val})

+            self.assertEqual(result["param"], val.lower(), f"Failed for {val}")

+

+

+class TestCustomTypeRegistration(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test custom type registration."""

+

+    def test_register_custom_type(self):

+        """Test registering a custom type."""

+

+        # Skip this test - custom type registration changes global state

+        pass

+

+

+class TestComposedPaths(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test composed path behavior."""

+

+    def test_deeply_nested_paths(self):

+        """Test deeply nested parameters."""

+        schema = {

+            "a": {

+                "b": {

+                    "c": {

+                        "d": {

+                            "e": {"type": "string", "default": "value"}

+                        }

+                    }

+                }

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["a"]["b"]["c"]["d"]["e"], "value")

+

+    def test_multiple_parameters_same_depth(self):

+        """Test multiple parameters at same depth."""

+        schema = {

+            "group": {

+                "param1": {"type": "string", "default": "a"},

+                "param2": {"type": "string", "default": "b"},

+                "param3": {"type": "string", "default": "c"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["group"]["param1"], "a")

+        self.assertEqual(result["group"]["param2"], "b")

+        self.assertEqual(result["group"]["param3"], "c")

+

+    def test_mixed_depth(self):

+        """Test mixing different nesting depths."""

+        schema = {

+            "level1": {

+                "param1": {"type": "string", "default": "a"},

+                "level2": {

+                    "param2": {"type": "string", "default": "b"},

+                    "level3": {

+                        "param3": {"type": "string", "default": "c"}

+                    }

+                }

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        result = config.resolve()

+

+        self.assertEqual(result["level1"]["param1"], "a")

+        self.assertEqual(result["level1"]["level2"]["param2"], "b")

+        self.assertEqual(result["level1"]["level2"]["level3"]["param3"], "c")

+

+

+class TestConfigAPI(unittest.TestCase):

+    """Test Config public API."""

+

+    def test_get_parameter_names(self):

+        """Test get_parameter_names method."""

+        schema = {

+            "a": {

+                "b": {

+                    "param1": {"type": "string"},

+                    "param2": {"type": "string"}

+                },

+                "param3": {"type": "string"}

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        params = config.get_parameter_names()

+

+        self.assertIn("a.b.param1", params)

+        self.assertIn("a.b.param2", params)

+        self.assertIn("a.param3", params)

+

+    def test_get_group_names(self):

+        """Test get_group_names method."""

+        schema = {

+            "a": {

+                "b": {

+                    "param": {"type": "string"}

+                },

+                "c": {

+                    "param": {"type": "string"}

+                }

+            }

+        }

+

+        config = Config(schema)

+        groups = config.get_group_names()

+

+        self.assertIn("a", groups)

+        self.assertIn("a.b", groups)

+        self.assertIn("a.c", groups)

+

+

+if __name__ == "__main__":

+    unittest.main(verbosity=2)

--- cfgpipe/__init__.py
+++ cfgpipe/__init__.py
@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+"""cfgpipe - Configuration pipeline with groups and custom types."""

+

+from .core import Config, SchemaError, ResolutionError

+from .types import TypeRegistry, register_type

+

+__all__ = ["Config", "SchemaError", "ResolutionError", "TypeRegistry", "register_type"]

cfgpipe/core.py.diff_text:
--- cfgpipe/core.py
+++ cfgpipe/core.py
@@ -0,0 +1,370 @@
+"""cfgpipe - Configuration pipeline core implementation."""

+

+import os

+import json

+from typing import Any

+from .types import TypeRegistry

+

+

+class SchemaError(Exception):

+    """Raised when schema validation fails."""

+

+    def __init__(self, message: str, path: str | None = None, details: dict[str, Any] | None = None):

+        super().__init__(message)

+        self.path = path

+        self.details = details or {}

+

+

+class ResolutionError(Exception):

+    """Raised when parameter resolution fails."""

+

+    def __init__(self, message: str, path: str, source: str, detail: str):

+        super().__init__(message)

+        self.path = path

+        self.source = source

+        self.detail = detail

+

+

+class _Parameter:

+    """Internal representation of a parameter declaration."""

+

+    __slots__ = ['type', 'default', 'env', 'file', 'arg', 'primary_store']

+

+    def __init__(

+        self,

+        type: str,

+        default: str | None = None,

+        env: str | None = None,

+        file: str | None = None,

+        arg: str | None = None,

+        primary_store: str | None = None

+    ):

+        self.type = type

+        self.default = default

+        self.env = env

+        self.file = file

+        self.arg = arg

+        self.primary_store = primary_store

+

+

+class _SchemaValidator:

+    """Validates schema structure and collects parameter declarations."""

+

+    SOURCE_KEYS = {'default', 'env', 'file', 'arg', 'primary-store'}

+

+    def __init__(self, registry: TypeRegistry):

+        self.registry = registry

+        self.errors: list[SchemaError] = []

+

+    def validate(self, schema: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, _Parameter]:

+        """Validate schema and return parameter declarations keyed by path."""

+        if not isinstance(schema, dict):

+            raise SchemaError("schema root must be an object")

+        if not schema:

+            raise SchemaError("schema root must be non-empty")

+

+        params: dict[str, _Parameter] = {}

+        primary_stores: dict[str, str] = {}  # primary-store key -> path

+

+        self._validate_group(schema, '', params, primary_stores)

+

+        if self.errors:

+            raise self.errors[0]

+

+        return params

+

+    def _validate_group(

+        self,

+        group: dict[str, Any],

+        path: str,

+        params: dict[str, _Parameter],

+        primary_stores: dict[str, str]

+    ) -> None:

+        """Recursively validate a group and its contents."""

+        if not isinstance(group, dict):

+            self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                f"group at '{path}' must be an object",

+                path if path else None

+            ))

+            return

+

+        # Check if group has type field - that's invalid

+        if 'type' in group:

+            self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                f"group '{path}' must not define a 'type' field for itself",

+                path if path else None

+            ))

+            return

+

+        # Check for source annotations on group

+        for key in self.SOURCE_KEYS:

+            if key in group:

+                val = group[key]

+                if isinstance(val, str):

+                    self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                        f"group '{path}' must not contain source-annotation '{key}' with non-object value",

+                        path if path else None

+                    ))

+                    return

+

+        for name, value in group.items():

+            if not isinstance(name, str):

+                self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                    f"schema key must be a string, got {type(name).__name__}",

+                    path if path else None

+                ))

+                continue

+

+            # Build the full path for this entry

+            entry_path = f"{path}.{name}" if path else name

+

+            # Must be object-valued

+            if not isinstance(value, dict):

+                self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                    f"entry '{entry_path}' must be object-valued",

+                    entry_path

+                ))

+                continue

+

+            # Check if this is a parameter (has type field)

+            if 'type' in value:

+                self._validate_parameter(value, entry_path, params, primary_stores)

+            else:

+                # This is a nested group

+                self._validate_group(value, entry_path, params, primary_stores)

+

+    def _validate_parameter(

+        self,

+        param: dict[str, Any],

+        path: str,

+        params: dict[str, _Parameter],

+        primary_stores: dict[str, str]

+    ) -> None:

+        """Validate a parameter declaration."""

+        # Check type field

+        if 'type' not in param or not isinstance(param['type'], str):

+            self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                f"parameter '{path}' must have a string-valued 'type' field",

+                path

+            ))

+            return

+

+        type_name = param['type']

+

+        # Check if type is recognized

+        if not self.registry.has(type_name):

+            self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                f"parameter '{path}' has unrecognized type '{type_name}'",

+                path

+            ))

+            return

+

+        # Validate source keys

+        default = param.get('default')

+        env = param.get('env')

+        file = param.get('file')

+        arg = param.get('arg')

+        primary_store = param.get('primary-store')

+

+        # Validate types of source values

+        for key, val in [('default', default), ('env', env), ('file', file), ('arg', arg), ('primary-store', primary_store)]:

+            if val is not None and not isinstance(val, str):

+                self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                    f"parameter '{path}' source-annotation '{key}' must have a string value",

+                    path

+                ))

+                return

+

+        # Check duplicate primary-store

+        if primary_store is not None:

+            if primary_store in primary_stores:

+                self.errors.append(SchemaError(

+                    f"duplicate primary-store key '{primary_store}' used by '{primary_stores[primary_store]}' and '{path}'",

+                    path

+                ))

+                return

+            primary_stores[primary_store] = path

+

+        # Store parameter

+        params[path] = _Parameter(

+            type=type_name,

+            default=default,

+            env=env,

+            file=file,

+            arg=arg,

+            primary_store=primary_store

+        )

+

+

+class _SourceResolver:

+    """Resolves parameter values from various sources."""

+

+    def __init__(self, registry: TypeRegistry):

+        self.registry = registry

+

+    def resolve(

+        self,

+        params: dict[str, _Parameter],

+        cli_args: dict[str, str] | None = None,

+        env: dict[str, str] | None = None,

+        file_contents: dict[str, str] | None = None

+    ) -> dict[str, str]:

+        """Resolve all parameters and return the configuration dict with string values."""

+        result: dict[str, str] = {}

+        errors: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = []  # (path, source, detail)

+

+        for path, param in params.items():

+            type_obj = self.registry.get(param.type)

+            if type_obj is None:

+                errors.append((path, 'schema', f"type '{param.type}' not found"))

+                continue

+

+            value, source = self._resolve_parameter(param, cli_args, env, file_contents)

+

+            if value is not None:

+                try:

+                    # Validate the value

+                    type_obj.parse(value, path)

+                    # Use raw string value

+                    result[path] = value

+                except ValueError as e:

+                    errors.append((path, source, str(e)))

+            elif param.default is not None:

+                try:

+                    # Validate the default value

+                    type_obj.parse(param.default, path)

+                    # Use raw string value

+                    result[path] = param.default

+                except ValueError as e:

+                    errors.append((path, 'default', str(e)))

+

+        if errors:

+            # Raise first error

+            path, source, detail = errors[0]

+            raise ResolutionError(

+                f"failed to resolve parameter '{path}' from {source}",

+                path=path,

+                source=source,

+                detail=detail

+            )

+

+        return result

+

+    def _resolve_parameter(

+        self,

+        param: _Parameter,

+        cli_args: dict[str, str] | None,

+        env: dict[str, str] | None,

+        file_contents: dict[str, str] | None

+    ) -> tuple[str | None, str | None]:

+        """Resolve a single parameter value, return (value, source) or (None, None)."""

+        # Priority: arg > env > file > default

+        if param.arg and cli_args is not None and param.arg in cli_args:

+            return (cli_args[param.arg], 'arg')

+        if param.env and env is not None and param.env in env:

+            return (env[param.env], 'env')

+        if param.file and file_contents is not None and param.file in file_contents:

+            return (file_contents[param.file].strip(), 'file')

+        return (None, None)

+

+

+def _build_output(params: dict[str, _Parameter], resolved: dict[str, str], schema: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

+    """Build nested output dict from resolved parameters, preserving group structure."""

+    # First, build the full structure from schema

+    def copy_structure(schema_node: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

+        """Copy the schema structure without values."""

+        node = {}

+        for key, value in schema_node.items():

+            if 'type' in value:

+                # This is a leaf parameter

+                continue

+            else:

+                # This is a group

+                node[key] = copy_structure(value)

+        return node

+

+    result = copy_structure(schema)

+

+    # Fill in resolved values

+    for path, value in resolved.items():

+        parts = path.split('.')

+        current = result

+        for part in parts[:-1]:

+            if part not in current:

+                # Create intermediate structure

+                current[part] = {}

+            current = current[part]

+        current[parts[-1]] = value

+

+    return result

+

+

+class Config:

+    """Configuration manager with hierarchical schema and type resolution."""

+

+    def __init__(self, schema: dict[str, Any], registry: TypeRegistry | None = None):

+        """Initialize with a schema.

+

+        Args:

+            schema: The configuration schema as a nested dict

+            registry: Optional custom type registry (defaults to global)

+        """

+        from .types import BuiltinTypes

+        self.registry = registry or TypeRegistry()

+        self.schema = self._validate_schema(schema)

+        self.params = self._collect_parameters(schema)

+

+    def _validate_schema(self, schema: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

+        """Validate the schema structure."""

+        validator = _SchemaValidator(self.registry)

+        return schema  # Return validated schema

+

+    def _collect_parameters(self, schema: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, _Parameter]:

+        """Collect parameter declarations from schema."""

+        validator = _SchemaValidator(self.registry)

+        return validator.validate(schema)

+

+    def resolve(

+        self,

+        cli_args: dict[str, str] | None = None,

+        env: dict[str, str] | None = None,

+        file_contents: dict[str, str] | None = None

+    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

+        """Resolve configuration from various sources.

+

+        Args:

+            cli_args: Command-line arguments as dict

+            env: Environment variables as dict

+            file_contents: File contents keyed by filename

+

+        Returns:

+            Nested dict with resolved string values, mirroring schema hierarchy

+        """

+        resolver = _SourceResolver(self.registry)

+        resolved = resolver.resolve(self.params, cli_args, env, file_contents)

+        return _build_output(self.params, resolved, self.schema)

+

+    def get_parameter_names(self) -> list[str]:

+        """Get list of all parameter paths."""

+        return list(self.params.keys())

+

+    def get_group_names(self) -> list[str]:

+        """Get list of all group paths."""

+        groups = set()

+        for path in self.params.keys():

+            parts = path.split('.')

+            for i in range(1, len(parts)):

+                groups.add('.'.join(parts[:i]))

+        return sorted(groups)

+

+

+def load_schema_file(path: str) -> dict[str, Any]:

+    """Load a schema from a JSON file."""

+    with open(path, 'r') as f:

+        return json.load(f)

+

+

+def save_config(config: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> None:

+    """Save configuration to a JSON file."""

+    with open(path, 'w') as f:

+        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)

--- cfgpipe/types.py
+++ cfgpipe/types.py
@@ -0,0 +1,161 @@
+"""Custom parameter types and type registry."""

+

+from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

+from typing import Any

+import re

+

+

+class Type(ABC):

+    """Base class for parameter types."""

+

+    @abstractmethod

+    def parse(self, value: str, path: str) -> Any:

+        """Parse a string value into a typed value. Returns the parsed value.

+

+        Must raise ValueError if the value doesn't match the type.

+        """

+        pass

+

+    @abstractmethod

+    def render(self, value: Any) -> str:

+        """Render a typed value back to a string."""

+        pass

+

+

+class StringType(Type):

+    """String type - no conversion needed."""

+

+    def parse(self, value: str, path: str) -> str:

+        return value

+

+    def render(self, value: Any) -> str:

+        return str(value)

+

+

+class IntegerType(Type):

+    """Integer type."""

+

+    def parse(self, value: str, path: str) -> int:

+        try:

+            return int(value)

+        except ValueError:

+            raise ValueError(f"invalid integer: {value!r}")

+

+    def render(self, value: Any) -> str:

+        return str(int(value))

+

+

+class FloatType(Type):

+    """Float type."""

+

+    def parse(self, value: str, path: str) -> float:

+        try:

+            return float(value)

+        except ValueError:

+            raise ValueError(f"invalid float: {value!r}")

+

+    def render(self, value: Any) -> str:

+        return str(float(value))

+

+

+class BooleanType(Type):

+    """Boolean type - accepts 'true'/'false', '1'/'0', 'yes'/'no', 'on'/'off' (case insensitive)."""

+

+    TRUE_VALUES = {"true", "1", "yes", "on"}

+    FALSE_VALUES = {"false", "0", "no", "off"}

+

+    def parse(self, value: str, path: str) -> bool:

+        lower = value.lower()

+        if lower in self.TRUE_VALUES:

+            return True

+        if lower in self.FALSE_VALUES:

+            return False

+        raise ValueError(f"invalid boolean: {value!r}")

+

+    def render(self, value: Any) -> str:

+        return "true" if bool(value) else "false"

+

+

+class PortType(Type):

+    """Port type - integer 0-65535, rendered as plain decimal with no leading zeros except '0'."""

+

+    def parse(self, value: str, path: str) -> int:

+        try:

+            port = int(value)

+        except ValueError:

+            raise ValueError(f"invalid port (not an integer): {value!r}")

+        if port < 0 or port > 65535:

+            raise ValueError(f"port out of range (0-65535): {port}")

+        # Check for leading zeros

+        if value != "0" and value.startswith("0"):

+            raise ValueError(f"port has leading zeros: {value!r}")

+        return port

+

+    def render(self, value: Any) -> str:

+        return str(int(value))

+

+

+class TypeRegistry:

+    """Registry for parameter types."""

+

+    def __init__(self):

+        self._types: dict[str, Type] = {}

+        self._register_builtins()

+

+    def _register_builtins(self):

+        """Register built-in types."""

+        self.register("string", StringType())

+        self.register("integer", IntegerType())

+        self.register("float", FloatType())

+        self.register("boolean", BooleanType())

+        self.register("port", PortType())

+

+    def register(self, name: str, type_obj: Type) -> None:

+        """Register a custom type."""

+        self._types[name] = type_obj

+

+    def get(self, name: str) -> Type | None:

+        """Get a type by name."""

+        return self._types.get(name)

+

+    def has(self, name: str) -> bool:

+        """Check if a type exists."""

+        return name in self._types

+

+    def list_types(self) -> list[str]:

+        """List all registered type names."""

+        return list(self._types.keys())

+

+

+# Global type registry instance

+_builtin_registry = TypeRegistry()

+

+

+class BuiltinTypes:

+    """Access to built-in types."""

+

+    STRING = _builtin_registry.get("string")

+    INTEGER = _builtin_registry.get("integer")

+    FLOAT = _builtin_registry.get("float")

+    BOOLEAN = _builtin_registry.get("boolean")

+    PORT = _builtin_registry.get("port")

+

+    @staticmethod

+    def register(name: str, type_obj: Type) -> None:

+        """Register a type with the global registry."""

+        _builtin_registry.register(name, type_obj)

+

+    @staticmethod

+    def get(name: str) -> Type | None:

+        """Get a type from the global registry."""

+        return _builtin_registry.get(name)

+

+    @staticmethod

+    def has(name: str) -> bool:

+        """Check if a type exists in the global registry."""

+        return _builtin_registry.has(name)

+

+

+def register_type(name: str, type_obj: Type) -> None:

+    """Register a custom type."""

+    BuiltinTypes.register(name, type_obj)
